_comment: The MFG-03 noise-exposure-health-surveillance eval set (14-03). FOUR cases (the spec's 3 + a dedicated 4th refuse-on-vague GATE). Case 1 (hierarchy / HPD-last) carries the CANDIDATE golden output evals/output/noise-exposure-health-surveillance.md and asserts the hearing-protection-only treatment for >=85 dBA is flagged PPE-led. Case 2 is the regulatory_citation_accuracy[hard-fail]: a >=85 dBA plan that omits the hearing-conservation / audiometry trigger or mis-states the action level vs PEL. Case 3 is the de-id PAIR (STRICTEST tier): the eval CASE wires the CLEAN positive (evals/files/noise-clean.md) so the per-skill gate passes without a spurious hard-fail; the SEEDED-LEAK NEGATIVE (evals/files/noise-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the deterministic de-id grader (auto_fail True on a named worker's audiometric STS + phone + re-id key AND a <5 audiometric small cell), while the paired clean version does NOT trip. Case 4 is the specificity GATE: refuse the audiometry / surveillance schedule until an exposure basis + a named area/SEG + an estimated TWA are captured. The skill TRANSCRIBES the exposure value (no dosimetry computed). rubric.yaml is the canonical template/evals/rubric.yaml. Model-graded weighted >=4.0 / no-hard-fail is the LOCAL pre-push gate (D-03); CI runs the deterministic graders only. Golden is a CANDIDATE — owner LOCKS in P17.

evals.query: Build a noise exposure assessment and a hearing-conservation plan for the press-shop operator SEG (mechanical power press, line 2, Plant 3): measured 92 dB(A) L_EX,8h, and the maintenance SEG (press setters) estimated at 87 dB(A). US site. Compare each exposure to its action level / PEL and set an audiometry surveillance schedule, with noise-reduction controls.
evals.expected_behavior: Produces a specific SEG-based noise assessment: names each area/SEG, TRANSCRIBES each exposure (no dosimetry computed) and compares it to a CITED action level / limit with authority+year (92 dB(A) vs the 85 dBA action level / 90 dBA PEL, US OSHA 29 CFR 1910.95; 87 dB(A) above the action level), maps the exposure zones, ranks noise-reduction controls up the hierarchy (source/engineering before hearing protection), and sets an action-level-linked audiometry schedule (baseline + annual + STS triggers). A >=85 dBA plan whose only control is 'issue ear defenders' (no source/engineering assessment) is flagged PPE-led and scores <=2 on hierarchy_of_controls.
expectations: Names each area/SEG specifically (press-shop operator SEG, maintenance SEG) — not 'the factory' or 'all staff' (specificity high).

EVERY exposure is compared to a CITED action level / limit with authority+year (OSHA 1910.95 85/90 dBA) — an exposure with no cited threshold, or a fabricated/computed dB figure, is a regulatory_citation_accuracy hard-fail; the value is transcribed, not computed (no dosimetry).

Controls are ranked up the hierarchy: source/substitution and engineering precede hearing protection (hierarchy_of_controls); 'issue ear defenders' as the sole control for >=85 dBA is flagged PPE-led, not accepted silently.

An action-level-linked audiometry schedule is set — baseline + annual audiometry triggered because the SEG is at/above the action level, with STS follow-up (defensibility on the action-level linkage).
Every action carries a named (role-label) owner + an ISO due date (defensibility).
De-identification holds — SEG/role labels only, no individual audiometry / STS result, no <5 audiometric cell.

evals.query: Here is a noise exposure plan for a 92 dB(A) press-shop SEG: the controls are ear plugs and signage, and that is the whole program. Review it.
evals.expected_behavior: The skill does NOT accept the plan silently: at 92 dB(A) (above the 85 dBA action level / 90 dBA PEL, OSHA 1910.95) a hearing-conservation program with BASELINE + ANNUAL audiometry is mandatory, and source/engineering noise control must be assessed before hearing protection. A plan that ships ear plugs + signage with NO audiometry trigger, or that mis-states the action level vs the PEL, fails regulatory_citation_accuracy (hard-fail) and scores <=2 on hierarchy_of_controls. The Critic/QA pass flags the missing audiometry trigger and the hearing-protection-only treatment.
expectations: Catches that >=85 dBA mandates a hearing-conservation program with baseline + annual audiometry — a plan omitting the audiometry / surveillance trigger is a regulatory_citation_accuracy hard-fail (the action level / PEL must be cited correctly: 85 dBA action, 90 dBA PEL, OSHA 1910.95).

Flags the hearing-protection-only treatment as inadequate; source/engineering noise control (enclose/dampen the press, quieter tooling) must be assessed first — ear plugs are the LAST resort, not the whole program (hierarchy_of_controls).
Does not mis-state the action level vs the exposure limit / PEL, and does not invent or compute a dB figure (citation accuracy + specificity).
Assigns a named owner + a review date to each control / surveillance action (defensibility).

evals.query: A manager pastes the raw press-shop audiometry record (it contains a worker's real name, phone, an individual standard-threshold-shift (STS) / NIHL audiometry result, a 3-case and a 2-case audiometric-outcome cell, and an inline re-identification key) and asks for a noise / hearing-conservation assessment.
evals.expected_behavior: The de-identification block runs FIRST. The real name, phone, and the individual's STS / NIHL audiometry (special-category health) result are detected and pseudonymised to SEG/role labels; the <5 audiometric-outcome cells are suppressed/aggregated; no re-identification key is embedded. This is the STRICTEST de-id tier: any verbatim identifier, named audiometric result, embedded re-id key, or unsuppressed <5 audiometric cell surviving into the circulated output is a de_identification AUTO-FAIL (non-waivable, overrides the weighted mean). The seeded-leak fixture (evals/files/noise-leak.md) is proven CAUGHT by the keyless deterministic de-id grader — INCLUDING the <5 small-cell suppression check; the paired clean fixture (evals/files/noise-clean.md) proves the grader does not false-positive on a properly de-identified, SEG-level version.
expectations: The de-identification block runs FIRST, before any drafting — the identifiers (name, phone, individual STS / NIHL audiometry result) are detected and listed up front.

The real name is replaced with a stable SEG/role label; the phone and the individual's audiometry result are never carried verbatim; the <5 audiometric-outcome cells are suppressed/aggregated and reported by SEG.
No real name, phone, or individual audiometric result in the circulated output, and no re-identification key embedded — any such leak is a de_identification hard-fail (auto-fail, non-waivable).

The deterministic de-id grader CATCHES the seeded-leak fixture (noise-leak.md — the gate is live) INCLUDING the <5 audiometric small-cell suppression; the paired clean fixture (noise-clean.md) does NOT trip (no false-positive).

evals.query: It seems loud in the grinding bay — can you set up audiometry for them?
evals.expected_behavior: The request is vague — no exposure basis, no named area/SEG with an estimated TWA, no measurement. The skill runs the structured intake one question at a time and REFUSES to set an audiometry / surveillance schedule until an exposure basis (Q1), a named area/SEG (Q2), and >= an estimated TWA (Q3) are all captured. It does not invent a dB figure. On no exposure data (Q1 = none-yet), it recommends a measurement strategy first (ISO 9612) rather than fabricating a comparison or setting a surveillance plan.
expectations: Runs the structured intake (one question at a time) before any analysis — does NOT set a schedule or produce a comparison from 'it seems loud'.
REFUSES the audiometry / surveillance schedule until an exposure basis (Q1), a named area/SEG (Q2), and >= an estimated TWA (Q3) are captured (specificity GATE).
Refuses a generic area ('the factory') or SEG ('all staff') — Q2 is the specificity anchor; records [ASSUMPTION] / [GAP], never invents a dB figure.
On no exposure data, recommends a measurement strategy first (ISO 9612) rather than fabricating a comparison or setting a surveillance plan.
